description:
To properly set the group owner of /etc/sysconfig/sshd, run the command: 
$ sudo chgrp root /etc/sysconfig/sshd
rationale:
The <tt>/etc/sysconfig/sshd</tt> file contains configuration options for the SSH daemon.
Protection of this file is important for system security. The file should be owned by the root
group to prevent unauthorized changes.
ocil_clause:
/etc/sysconfig/sshd does not have a group owner of
root
ocil:
To check the group ownership of /etc/sysconfig/sshd, run the command: 
$ ls -lL /etc/sysconfig/sshd
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following group-owner: root
fixtext:
Change the group of the file /etc/sysconfig/sshd to root by running the following command:
$ sudo chgrp root /etc/sysconfig/sshd
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /etc/sysconfig/sshd file must be group-owned by root.
title: Verify Group Who Owns /etc/sysconfig/sshd File
